summary.note: WR calculation approximate — 3 case groups have mixed outcomes (2W/1L or similar)

key_findings: Extreme probs have ~73% WR vs 62.7% paper / 55.6% live overall WR — they are NOT systematically wrong. Shutting them all down would hurt performance.
15/25 cases (60%) are LEGITIMATELY EXTREME: market already priced at 80%+ and model agrees within 15pp, OR multi-runner market where 5-10% is mathematically appropriate (golf Top-N, correct score).

4/25 are RESULT CONTAMINATION: model saw same-match result in key_facts and anchored on it. Of these, 2 were correct (Arsenal EPL winner on final day; Philippines v Guam same-day result) and 2 were wrong (Zverev 95% → Darderi won; Nguyen Tan 95% skipped). The correct ones 'won' only because the result was already known.
2/25 are IN-PLAY/LIVE SCORE contamination: Ribeiro/Zeballos (match running) and Irish politics (wrong constituency referenced). In-play guard (MOD-241) now addresses these.
Tennis is the worst sport for result contamination (2/5 extreme-prob tennis signals contaminated). Tavily fetches H2H/match pages that contain current tournament results.
The divergence gate (MOD-241, 5pp min) does NOT help here because result contamination produces very HIGH divergence (37pp for Nguyen Tan) which looks like a genuine signal.
The research quality gate (MOD-241) does NOT help because tennisstats/sofascore/flashscore are legitimate sites not in junk blocklist.

England W v Ireland W (52pp edge, 50% market) is the most anomalous case — WON but root cause unclear. May be a 'Completed Match' market type where the model correctly assessed near-certainty the match would complete.

fix_recommendations.fix: Prompt-level probability cap for 2-runner match odds markets
fix_recommendations.detail: In analyze_research.py Claude prompt: add instruction 'For a 2-runner match odds market, do not exceed 87% for either runner unless the research explicitly confirms one player/team cannot participate (injury withdrawal, forfeit, etc.). Competitive sporting outcomes always carry uncertainty.' This caps Nguyen Tan type cases at 87% max, reducing Wang-amplified edge from 37pp to ~20pp, which the divergence gate would still pass but would prevent extreme anchoring.
fix_recommendations.impact: medium — prevents 95%+ outputs on 2-runner markets, still allows strong legitimate signals

fix_recommendations.detail: Add to Claude prompt: 'IMPORTANT: If the research contains a completed match result for the EXACT same two competitors you are assessing (e.g. "Team A beat Team B 6-3 7-5 on [today or yesterday's date]"), you MUST: (1) state this in contrary_evidence as "RESULT_CONTAMINATION: [result found]", (2) set your probability no higher than 72% for the predicted winner (the market already prices in known outcomes), (3) base your estimate on FORM and RANKINGS only, ignoring the contaminating result. A result from the current tournament or the last 48 hours is NOT predictive evidence — it IS the outcome you are trying to predict.'
fix_recommendations.impact: high — directly addresses the Arsenal EPL, Philippines v Guam, Nguyen Tan, Zverev cases

fix_recommendations.fix: Post-research key_fact scanner in analyze_research.py / digest.py
fix_recommendations.detail: After building key_facts, scan for: (1) both competitor names mentioned + score pattern (\d-\d) + today or yesterday's date. If found, set brief_market['_result_contamination'] = True. In digest.py, if _result_contamination=True AND model_prob > 87%, suppress the signal with a log: 'Result contamination detected: suppressing extreme prob signal for [event]'. This is a belt-and-braces fallback if the prompt instruction fails.
fix_recommendations.effort: medium — regex scan on key_facts, new flag, digest filter
fix_recommendations.impact: high — catches cases where prompt instruction is ignored

fix_recommendations.fix: Require explicit 'result_found' field in Claude JSON output
fix_recommendations.detail: Add to the JSON output schema Claude must return: '"result_found": null or "[description of any completed match result found in research for these exact competitors]"'. This forces Claude to surface result contamination rather than silently using it. If result_found is non-null, log it to the brief and apply the probability cap.
fix_recommendations.effort: medium — prompt schema change + extraction code
fix_recommendations.impact: medium — improves auditability even if model still anchors

fix_recommendations.fix: Tennis-specific query rewrite to avoid match result pages
fix_recommendations.detail: In researcher.py tennis _build_queries(), currently includes '{player_a} vs {player_b} match preview {year}'. Rewrite to: '{player_a} vs {player_b} {tournament} preview form analysis' and add explicit query for 'site:atptour.com OR site:wtatennis.com {player_a} {year} season stats'. This encourages Tavily to return official tour pages with season stats rather than live-score aggregators that have current tournament results.
fix_recommendations.effort: medium — query redesign, test across 5+ briefs
fix_recommendations.impact: medium — reduces chance of fetching result pages at source

fix_recommendations.fix: Do NOT suppress all extreme-prob signals
fix_recommendations.detail: Analysis shows extreme prob signals have ~73% WR vs 62.7% overall. The legitimate cases (market already 85%+, multi-runner markets) are profitable. A blanket extreme-prob filter would REDUCE performance. Fixes should be targeted at result contamination only, not all extreme outputs.
fix_recommendations.impact: negative if ignored — do not implement a global extreme-prob filter

cases.notes: Market already 92%, model agrees within 15pp
Market already 96%, model agrees within 15pp

contaminating_key_facts: Restore Britain's chances of receiving 10%+ are low.
cases.notes: Prediction-style key_fact, not a result. Model correctly assessed low likelihood.

cases.event_name: England W v Ireland W (Completed Match)
cases.notes: 52pp edge vs 50% market — extremely high. No brief match found for this specific market. Could be result contamination or legitimately strong form signal. Resolved WIN.
No result contamination found. Model may have had good form data or this is multi-runner market logic.

contaminating_key_facts: Arsenal won the English Premier League 2025/26. The season concluded on May 6, 2026. No other team made a serious challenge.
cases.notes: KEY FACT SAYS ARSENAL WON ON THIS EXACT DATE. Model saw the result and output 91%. Outcome: WIN (obviously — result was known). Market at 78.74% was already pricing it in. This is result contamination that happened to be correct.

contaminating_key_facts: Jon Rahm is currently the favorite (no result stated)
cases.notes: Multi-runner golf Winner market. 5% LAY on outsider at 16.7% market price is reasonable. No result in key_facts.

cases.event_name: Irish Politics - Mark Lohan Sinn Fein
contaminating_key_facts: Dublin Central by-election yet to occur — different constituency
cases.notes: Key fact referenced wrong constituency (Dublin Central vs Galway West). Multi-candidate election, 9.79% on one candidate is plausible. WIN.

cases.event_name: West Ham vs Arsenal - Correct Score x3
cases.notes: 3× correct score lays (0-1, 0-3, 1-1) each at 5.7% vs 16.7% market. Standard multi-runner correct score market. 2 won, 1 lost.
No brief available. Liverpool finished Top 6 — model was wrong. But 5% LAY on a 16.7% market may be multi-runner logic.

contaminating_key_facts: Alexander Zverev defeated Luciano Darderi in their last meeting on clay in May 2024. Zverev leads H2H 1-0.
cases.notes: H2H result from 2024 used to anchor at 95%. Darderi won — model was wrong. Classic result contamination: old result treated as strongly predictive of current match.

cases.event_name: USPGA Top-N lays x3 (DeChambeau, Young, Aberg)
cases.notes: Multi-runner Top-N golf markets. 5% LAY on non-favourite in 6+ runner field is defensible.
No brief available. Market at 88.5%, model at 95% — only 6.5pp divergence. Legitimately lopsided match (Basilashvili ranked much lower).
Market at 91.7%, model at 95% — agrees within 4pp. H2H data is legitimate form, not result contamination. WIN.

contaminating_key_facts: 'most recent match was on 16 May 2026' — same day as trade
cases.notes: Key fact mentions match on 16 May 2026 (trade date). Could be result contamination, but market at 85% and model at 92.5% is only 7.5pp divergence. WIN — classifying as legitimately extreme since market already priced heavily.

cases.event_name: Newcastle vs West Ham - Newcastle BACK
cases.notes: Same-day reference in key_facts but market at 87%, model at 94.1% — 7pp divergence. WIN.

contaminating_key_facts: Arsenal has won 14 times, Burnley once, 2-0 in Nov 2025
cases.notes: Market at 89%, model at 95%. Historical H2H data (not same-match result). LOSS — Arsenal didn't win despite 95% model. Could be legitimately unlucky at 89% market price.
Multi-runner market. WIN.
Multi-runner Top-N. Aaron Rai finished Top 5 — model was wrong. But 5% LAY at 16.7% is statistically defensible.

contaminating_key_facts: The Philippines defeated Guam 4-0 in their 2026 international friendly on June 3, 2026
Philippines has dominated Guam winning all 4 recent matches
cases.notes: CRITICAL: Key fact states Philippines defeated Guam 4-0 on June 3 2026 — the exact match date. Model saw the result, output 95%. Market was already 92.59%. WIN — but this is post-hoc result use, not genuine prediction.
Market at 84%, model at 91%. No result contamination. WIN.

contaminating_key_facts: Match already in-play at slip time (started 20:10 UTC)
cases.notes: In-play guard now deployed (MOD-241). Match was live when slip fired.

contaminating_key_facts: Lucie Nguyen Tan defeated Despina Papamichail on 18.06.2026 in Blois, France
Nguyen Tan defeated Papamichail 6-3, 7-5 in W75 Blois 2026 final on 18 June 2026
Flashscore: 1-0 Papamichail D. (live score from QF)
cases.notes: Yesterday's tournament result contaminated today's QF assessment. Contrary_evidence correctly flagged date conflict but model had already anchored. Skipped by operator.
